ACCEPTANCE: whole-lemma-block and whole-restriction-block byte parity for every lemma/restriction in the curated set, asserted in tests; probe-pinned laws in BEHAVIOR.md for every glyph/precedence/wrap decision; all cargo tests green (R1/R2 suites untouched); zero warnings; UNOBSERVABLE register for anything unreachable. 1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

2	
3	Build a standalone Rust crate `pretty-clean` in `workspace/` that reimplements,
4	from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, the tamarin-prover binary's **theory echo** — the
5	`theory <name> begin … end` text it prints at theory-load. Given a
6	parsed/closed theory (data model in `interface/ast_types.rs`), produce
7	byte-identical render text and the API in `interface/required_api.md`.
8	
9	## The observable boundary
10	
11	`oracle/pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy>` runs the Haskell binary **WITHOUT
12	`--prove`** and prints the EXTRACTED THEORY ECHO — exactly the bytes you must
13	reproduce. Running without `--prove` is deliberate and load-bearing:
14	
15	* the echo prints at theory-load (~0.25 s/file), so the gate is fast; and
16	* **no proof search runs**, so every lemma renders `by sorry` and NO proof
17	  tree is emitted. The proof/constraint-system renderer is solver output, not
18	  pretty-printer surface, and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE (see "Stays ported").
19	
20	The extracted block is `theory <name> begin … end` MINUS two trailing
21	formal-comment blocks the tool appends inside that span and everything after
22	`end`:
23	
24	* the wellformedness report (`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`
25	  or the multi-line `/*\nWARNING …\n*/`) — a **separate slice** owned by the
26	  wellformedness cluster; do not reimplement it here;
27	* the volatile `/*\nGenerated from: …\n*/` build stamp (version / git rev /
28	  compile time); and
29	* the `====… summary of summaries …` after `end` (carries processing time).
30	
31	Interior comments — per-rule `variants (modulo AC)` blocks, `has exactly the
32	trivial AC variant`, and `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples`
33	— are KEPT: they are your output. The extraction awk is embedded in the oracle
34	and in the acceptance gate identically, so
35	`diff <(pretty_oracle.sh f) <(pretty-clean-render f)` == the gate verdict.
36	
37	## Acceptance gate (full corpus, primary oracle AND integration check)
38	
39	`/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/pretty_gate.sh` runs the binary at `RS_PATH`
40	(point it at your build) no-prove over the whole 419-file corpus, extracts the
41	theory echo, and diffs it against a no-prove Haskell reference cache
42	(`scripts/.hs_pretty_cache`, auto-filled on first run). It reuses the batch
43	gate's `ckey`/`flags`/`strip_env` machinery so per-file canonical flags stay
44	identical. Output TSV: `relpath  MATCH|DIFF|SKIP_*  diffcount`. Warm-cache full
45	corpus ≈ 72 s at JOBS=6. Run it every iteration; `ALLOWLIST` subsets it while
46	you work a single sub-target. **Acceptance is the FULL-corpus gate green, not a

52	pure-render surface is at 100% today, so your target is: keep every one of the
53	403 green while the ported renderer is deleted underneath you.
54	
55	## Sub-target decomposition (each independently gate-checkable via ALLOWLIST)
56	
57	Ranked by (individual-author yield ÷ isolation) — a good sealed round takes ONE
58	cleanly-observable sub-component at a time. Corpus counts are over the 419-file

62	|---|------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|------------------|

81	fitting logic from the GPL side. Your job is the tamarin-specific part: WHICH
82	combinators, in what nesting, with what literal strings and glyphs.

84	## Solver-entangled inputs — pure render vs. "stays ported"
85	
86	The renderer is a `Theory → text` function ONLY for the surface below. Some
87	displayed data is COMPUTED by the ported closure/solver; the clean crate
88	RENDERS it but is handed it pre-computed, and the computation stays ported.
89	
90	CLEANLY REIMPLEMENTABLE (pure render — R1–R4):
91	* term rendering; signature block; facts; rules; macros/predicates;
92	  formula & restriction & lemma-statement rendering; the guarded-formula
93	  comment TEXT (given the guarded formula); the `variants (modulo AC)` block

97	* **Proof trees / proof methods / constraint-system pane** (`pretty_system.rs`,

225	## Rule blocks (R2)
226	
227	Curated byte targets: round2/targets/*.hs.txt (12 corpus files, 72 rule
228	blocks, every block byte-verified by tests/round2_rules.rs round-trip
229	parity). A rule BLOCK is:
230	
231	```
232	rule (modulo E) Name[attrs]:
233	   <body>
234	<blank line>
235	  // loop breaker(s): [..]        (optional, col 2)
236	  <variants comment>              (col 2)
237	```

239	### Header
240	
241	* `rule (modulo E) Name:` — the modulo annotation is input data (`E` on the
242	  closed rule, `AC` on the variant re-render inside the comment).
243	* Attribute list attaches to the name: `Name[color=#abcdef, no_derivcheck,
244	  issapicrule, role='r']:` — canonical order color < no_derivcheck <
245	  issapicrule < role regardless of source order; the LAST color/role
246	  declaration wins; `process='…'` and external `x-…` attributes are DROPPED
247	  (probe:p_rattr, target:issue713). Spellings: `color=#<hex>` unquoted
248	  (lowercased upstream: '#AbCdEf' → `#abcdef`), `role='…'` quoted.

2	// type surface of the parsed-theory data model the THEORY PRETTY-PRINTER

1	# Required public API (interoperability surface) — pretty-clean
2	
3	The clean crate must expose these entry points so the open side can wire it in
4	behind thin adapters that translate the live `tamarin_parser::ast` /
5	closed-theory values into the crate's own AST subset. Every function returns
6	text that is byte-identical to the oracle's no-prove theory echo for the
7	corresponding fragment. Names are indicative; the boundary that matters is
8	"one entry point per observable sub-target, each independently gate-checkable".
9	
10	```rust
11	// ── (top) whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end` (minus the wf
12	//    comment and the Generated-from stamp, which are appended by callers). ──
13	pub fn render_theory(thy: &Theory, sig: &Signature) -> String;
14	
15	// ── (a) signature block: builtins / functions / equations declarations ──
16	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &Signature) -> String;
17	
18	// ── (b) macros / predicates blocks ──
19	pub fn render_macros(macros: &[Macro]) -> String;        // "macros: …"
20	pub fn render_predicates(preds: &[Predicate]) -> String; // "predicates: …"
21	
22	// ── (c) rule rendering: one multiset-rewrite rule, plus fact & TERM render ──
23	pub fn render_rule(rule: &Rule, variants: Option<&AcVariants>) -> String;
24	pub fn render_fact(fact: &Fact) -> String;               // `Foo( a, b )`
25	pub fn render_term(term: &Term) -> String;               // THE deep core
26	
27	// ── (d) restriction / lemma FORMULA rendering ──
28	pub fn render_restriction(r: &Restriction) -> String;
29	pub fn render_lemma(l: &Lemma, guarded: Option<&Guarded>) -> String;
30	pub fn render_formula(f: &Formula) -> String;            // ∀ ∃ ⇒ ∧ ∨ ¬ @ ⊏ …
31	
32	// Layout primitive (BSD-provenance, NOT part of the GPL-erasure surface):
33	// the HughesPJ `Doc` engine + combinators. Reuse the graphdot cluster's
34	// clean-room engine (workspace/graph-clean/src/pretty.rs, derived from
35	// sanctioned/pretty-1.1.3.6). Width = 110, ribbon = 73 for the theory echo.
36	```
37	
38	`Theory`, `Term`, `Fact`, `Formula`, `Atom`, `Rule`, `Lemma`, `Restriction`,
39	`Macro`, `Predicate`, `VarSpec`, `SortHint`, `BinOp`: see `ast_types.rs`.
40	
41	`Signature`, `AcVariants`, `Guarded` are pre-computed inputs supplied by the
42	ported closure/solver (SPEC.md "Solver-entangled inputs"); the clean crate
43	only RENDERS them, and their observable text is learned from the oracle.

1	//! pretty-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover theory
2	//! pretty-printer (the `theory <name> begin … end` echo), derived purely from
3	//! black-box oracle behavior. See ../../SPEC.md for the observable boundary and
4	//! the R1–R4 sub-target decomposition, and workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the
5	//! inferred behavioral spec you build up as you probe the oracle.
6	//!
7	//! SCAFFOLD ONLY. Every render entry point below is an UNIMPLEMENTED stub so
8	//! the crate compiles and the test harness runs (and fails meaningfully) from
9	//! day one. Fill in bodies one sub-target at a time, gating each against
10	//! `scripts/pretty_gate.sh` with an ALLOWLIST (see round1/).
11	//!
12	//! Recommended order: R1 (term + signature) → R2 (rules) → R3 (formula /
13	//! lemma / restriction) → R4 (macros / predicates).
14	
15	pub mod ast;
16	pub mod doc; // BSD HughesPJ Doc engine — reuse graphdot's clean-room port.
17	
18	pub mod formula; // R3
19	pub mod lemma; // R3
20	pub mod macros; // R4
21	pub mod rule; // R2
22	pub mod signature; // R1
23	pub mod term; // R1 (deep core)
24	pub mod theory; // top-level assembly
25	
26	pub use ast::*;
27	
28	/// Whole theory echo: `theory <name> begin … end`, minus the wf comment and
29	/// the Generated-from stamp (both appended by the caller / stripped by the
30	/// gate). Top-level assembly of the R1–R4 sub-targets. See `theory::render`.
31	pub fn render_theory(thy: &ast::Theory, sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
32	    theory::render(thy, sig)
33	}
34	
35	/// R1: the signature section — header comment, blank line, then the
36	/// `builtins:` / `functions:` / `equations:` declarations. See
37	/// `signature::render`.
38	pub fn render_signature_block(sig: &ast::Signature) -> String {
39	    signature::render(sig)
40	}
41	
42	/// R1: one term, rendered at the echo's layout parameters. See `term::render`.
43	pub fn render_term(term: &ast::Term) -> String {
44	    term::render(term)
45	}
46	
47	/// R2: one whole rule block — header, body, blank line, loop-breaker line,
48	/// and the AC-variants comment (`None` renders the trivial-variant
49	/// one-liner). See `rule::render`.
50	pub fn render_rule(r: &ast::Rule, variants: Option<&ast::AcVariants>) -> String {
51	    rule::render(r, variants)
52	}
53	
54	/// R2: one fact (`!Name( a, b )[+]`). See `rule::render_fact`.
55	pub fn render_fact(f: &ast::Fact) -> String {
56	    rule::render_fact(f)
57	}

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Black-box oracle for the THEORY PRETTY-PRINTER clean-room.
3	#
4	# Runs the sanctioned Haskell tamarin-prover on a .spthy file WITHOUT --prove
5	# (the theory echo prints at theory-load; no proof search) and, by default,
6	# prints the EXTRACTED THEORY-ECHO BLOCK — the exact `theory <name> begin …
7	# end` text your reimplementation must reproduce byte-for-byte.
8	#
9	#   pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [extra args]   # -> extracted theory echo
10	#   RAW=1 pretty_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [args]   # -> full HS output (2>&1),

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Materialize the Haskell reference THEORY ECHO for each round-1 target into
# round2/targets/<safe>.hs.txt, using the sanctioned HS oracle only. Run from
# inside the sealed workspace; touches only the HS binary (via pretty_oracle.sh)
# and the corpus .spthy inputs — both permitted materials.
set -u
here=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
CORPUS="${CORPUS:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples}"
FLAGS_MAP="${FLAGS_MAP:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/file_flags.tsv}"
ORACLE="$here/../oracle/pretty_oracle.sh"
mkdir -p "$here/targets"
n=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r _tags REL; do
  case "$_tags" in \#*|"") continue;; esac
  f="$CORPUS/$REL"
  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING $REL" >&2; continue; }
  fl=$(awk -F'\t' -v r="$REL" '!/^#/ && $1==r {print $2}' "$FLAGS_MAP"); fl=${fl//@cd/}
  safe=$(printf '%s' "$REL" | tr '/' '_')
  # pretty_oracle.sh runs HS no-prove and prints the extracted theory echo.
  # shellcheck disable=SC2086
  "$ORACLE" "$f" $fl > "$here/targets/$safe.hs.txt" && n=$((n+1))
done < "$here/families.tsv"
echo "wrote $n HS reference theory-echo blocks to targets/"

theory Minimal_Loop_Example

begin

// Function signature and definition of the equational theory E

functions: fst/1, pair/2, snd/1
equations: fst(<x.1, x.2>) = x.1, snd(<x.1, x.2>) = x.2

/* looping facts with injective instances: A/1 */

rule (modulo E) Start:
   [ Fr( x ) ] --[ Start( x ) ]-> [ A( x ) ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */

rule (modulo E) Loop:
   [ A( x ) ] --[ Loop( x ) ]-> [ A( x ) ]

  // loop breaker: [0]
  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */

rule (modulo E) Stop:
   [ A( x ) ] --[ Stop( x ) ]-> [ ]

  /* has exactly the trivial AC variant */

lemma Start_before_Loop [reuse, use_induction]:
  all-traces
  "∀ x #j. (Loop( x ) @ #j) ⇒ (∃ #i. (Start( x ) @ #i) ∧ (#i < #j))"
/*
guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:
"∃ x #j. (Loop( x ) @ #j) ∧ ∀ #i. (Start( x ) @ #i) ⇒ ¬(#i < #j)"
*/
by sorry

lemma Start_before_Stop:
  all-traces
  "∀ x #j. (Stop( x ) @ #j) ⇒ (∃ #i. (Start( x ) @ #i) ∧ (#i < #j))"
/*
guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:
"∃ x #j. (Stop( x ) @ #j) ∧ ∀ #i. (Start( x ) @ #i) ⇒ ¬(#i < #j)"
*/
by sorry

lemma Loop_before_Stop [reuse, use_induction]:
  all-traces "∀ x #i #j. ((Stop( x ) @ #j) ∧ (Loop( x ) @ #i)) ⇒ (#i < #j)"
/*
guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:
"∃ x #i #j. (Stop( x ) @ #j) ∧ (Loop( x ) @ #i) ∧ ¬(#i < #j)"
*/
by sorry

lemma Stop_unique [reuse]:
  all-traces "∀ x #i #j. ((Stop( x ) @ #j) ∧ (Stop( x ) @ #i)) ⇒ (#i = #j)"
/*
guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:
"∃ x #i #j. (Stop( x ) @ #j) ∧ (Stop( x ) @ #i) ∧ ¬(#i = #j)"
*/
by sorry

# Per-file canonical extra HS flags, beyond bare `--prove`.
#
# WHY: some theories are meant to be run with specific flags (Makefile
# case-study recipes / in-file invocation comments). Running them bare makes
# HS time out or produce NO output (SKIP), so we never recorded the real HS
# behaviour and "lost track" of what they need. This map records the canonical
# flags so the HS cache is generated the right way, and the SAME flags are
# passed to RS for the comparison.
#
# Format: <relpath><TAB><flags>   (relpath under examples/; '#' lines ignored).
# Consumed by corpus_file_diff.sh and diff_proof_raw.sh. The cache key is
# salted with a hash of the flags, so flagged entries are distinct from the
# bare cache (flagless files are completely unaffected — no cache churn).
#
# NOTE: `--stop-on-trace=dfs` is the DEFAULT (HS `constructAutoProver`:
# `fromMaybe CutDFS`), so bare `--prove` already == dfs — do NOT list it here.
#
# RS-MATCHABILITY:
#   --diff               RS NOT PORTED (errors "not yet ported"); HS recorded,
#                        RS can't match → kept out of parity_corpus.txt.
90 /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/file_flags.tsv

theory function_application begin

functions: true/0,eq/2
equations: eq(x,x)=x

predicate: True(x) <=> (x = true())

rule A:
  [In(x)] --[ _restrict(True(eq(x,x))) ]-> []

---ACC---
/*
 * Protocol: Centralized Monitor
 * Modeler:  Kevin Morio and Robert Künnemann
 * Date:     Nov 2019
 * Source:   "Automated Verification of Accountability in Security Protocols", Robert Künnemann, Ilkan Esiyok, and Michael Backes, CSF19
 * Status:   working
 */

theory CentralizedMonitor
begin

functions:
  pk/1, sk/1[private],
  sig/2, verify/3,
  true/0,
  extract/1,
  NormalReq/0, SpecialReq/0

equations:
  verify(sig(m, sk(i)), m, pk(sk(i))) = true,
  extract(sig(m, sk(i))) = m

predicates:
  IsSpecial(a)  <=> a = SpecialReq,
  IsNormal(a)   <=> a = NormalReq,
  IsValid(a)    <=>     a = SpecialReq | a = NormalReq,
  IsInvalid(a)  <=> not(a = SpecialReq | a = NormalReq),
  IsPair(m)     <=> Ex a b. m = <a, b>


let D =
  insert <'roles', $x, 'D'>, true;
  out(pk(sk($x)));
  in(a);
  let m_1 = <'Do', a>
      m_2 = <'Permit', a>
  in
    if IsNormal(a) then
      out(<m_1, sig(m_1, sk($x))>)
    else if IsSpecial(a) then
      out(<m_2, sig(m_2, sk($x))>)

let C =
  insert <'roles', $y, 'C'>, true;
  out(pk(sk($y)));
  in($x);
  in(<<'Permit', a>, sig_m_2>);
  let m_2 = <'Permit', a>
      m_3 = <m_2, sig_m_2>
  in
    lookup <'roles', $x, 'D'> as is_d in
      if verify(sig_m_2, m_2, pk(sk($x))) = true then
        if IsSpecial(a) then
          out(<m_3, sig(m_3, sk($y))>)

let M =
  ( in(<<'Do', a>, sig_m_1>);
    let m_1 = <'Do', a> in
      if verify(sig_m_1, m_1, pk(sk($x))) = true then
        lookup <'roles', $x, 'D'> as is_d in
          new id;
          event Log($x, a, id);
          event Execute(a, id)
  )
  +
  ( in(<<<'Permit', a>, sig_m_2>, sig_m_3>);
    let m_2 = <'Permit', a>
        m_3 = <m_2, sig_m_2>
    in
      if verify(sig_m_3, m_3, pk(sk($y))) = true then
        if verify(sig_m_2, m_2, pk(sk($x))) = true then
          lookup <'roles', $y, 'C'> as is_c in
            lookup <'roles', $x, 'D'> as is_d in
              new id;
              event Log(<$x, $y>, a, id);
              event Execute(a, id)
  )

let Adv =
  in('c', <'corrupt', $x>);

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Materialize the Haskell reference THEORY ECHO for each round-3 target into
# round3/targets/<safe>.hs.txt, using the sanctioned HS oracle only. Run from
# inside the sealed workspace; touches only the HS binary (via pretty_oracle.sh)
# and the corpus .spthy inputs — both permitted materials.
set -u
here=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
CORPUS="${CORPUS:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples}"
FLAGS_MAP="${FLAGS_MAP:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/file_flags.tsv}"
ORACLE="$here/../oracle/pretty_oracle.sh"
mkdir -p "$here/targets"
n=0
while IFS=$'\t' read -r _tags REL; do
  case "$_tags" in \#*|"") continue;; esac
  f="$CORPUS/$REL"
  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING $REL" >&2; continue; }
  fl=$(awk -F'\t' -v r="$REL" '!/^#/ && $1==r {print $2}' "$FLAGS_MAP"); fl=${fl//@cd/}
  safe=$(printf '%s' "$REL" | tr '/' '_')
  # pretty_oracle.sh runs HS no-prove and prints the extracted theory echo.
  # shellcheck disable=SC2086
  "$ORACLE" "$f" $fl > "$here/targets/$safe.hs.txt" && n=$((n+1))
done < "$here/families.tsv"
echo "wrote $n HS reference theory-echo blocks to targets/"

input.new_string:
## Formula rendering (R3)

Curated byte targets: round3/targets/*.hs.txt (20 corpus files; 84
restriction + 139 lemma blocks, every block byte-verified by
tests/round3_formulas.rs round-trip parity).

### Glyphs and atoms

| construct | rendered | provenance |
|---|---|---|
| true / false | `⊤` / `⊥` | probe:q_w1 |
| conjunction / disjunction | `∧` / `∨` | probe:q_at1, q_p2 |
| implication / iff | `⇒` / `⇔` | probe:q_p2, q_r2 |
| negation | `¬(…)` — argument ALWAYS parenthesized | probe:q_p2 s8 |
| quantifiers | `∀` / `∃` | everywhere |
| action atom | `Fact( … ) @ tp` — spaces around `@` | probe:q_at1 |
| temporal/nat order | `t1 < t2` | probe:q_at1 |
| equality | `t1 = t2` | probe:q_at1 |
| subterm | `t1 ⊏ t2` (source `<<` and `⊏` both echo `⊏`) | probe:q_at1, target:NumberSubtermTests |
| last | `last(tp)` — NO interior spaces (unlike facts) | probe:q_at1 |

Terms inside atoms render through the R1 term core unchanged (probe:q_l4 —
pair trailing-`", "` wrap, AC self-parens); action facts through the R2 fact
construction unchanged, `!` prefix included (target:NSLPK3 `!KU( ni ) @ #j`,
probe:q_l3).

### Parenthesization

* EVERY operand of a binary connective (∧ ∨ ⇒ ⇔) is wrapped in `(…)`,
  whatever it is — atom, ⊤/⊥, ¬, quantifier, another connective
  (probe:q_p2 s1–s13; targets NSLPK3/Cronto/acc).
* `¬`'s argument is always wrapped (double wrap when ¬ is itself an
  operand: `(¬(x = 'd'))`).
* Quantifier BODIES and the top level are bare (probe:q_p2, q_w1).
* Chains render their source association faithfully — `a | b | c` parses
  left-nested upstream and echoes `((a) ∨ (b)) ∨ (c)`; no flattening,
  no re-association (probe:q_at1, probe:q_p2 s3–s6).

### Layout

* Binary connective = `sep [lhs-operand <+> glyph, rhs-operand]`: one line
  `(A) ∧ (B)` when it fits; otherwise the glyph stays on the lhs' LAST line
  and the rhs drops to the group origin (targets NSLPK3 types /
  Yubikey slightly_weaker_invariant / Cronto notSameRole deep left chain).
* Quantifier = `sep [glyph<>" "<>fsep binders<>".", nest 1 body]`:
  - binders are space-separated in source order with their R1 sigils and
    `.idx` suffixes (`∀ x.1 a.1 #i.1.` — probe:q_b1, target:acc lemmas);
  - the binder fill wraps aligned after `"∀ "` (origin+2 — probe:q_l2 bw1);
  - the `.` attaches to the last binder;
  - the body sits beside after one space, or drops to (quantifier origin+1)
    — the nest 1 (probe:q_l2 bw1/bw2; paren-nested `(((∃ #j.` → body at
    paren col + 2 confirms the same nest at depth).
* Relation atoms (`=` `<` `⊏`) = `sep [lhs-term <+> glyph, rhs-term]`: glyph
  attached to the lhs line, rhs drops to the atom origin (probe:q_l4 tw1–3).
* Action atom = `hsep [fact, "@", timepoint]`: `@ tp` NEVER drops alone —
  at overflow the FACT breaks internally (its `)` drops to the fact column,
  `@ tp` beside it: `) @ #i` — probe:q_l5 m63/m64 pins hsep over the
  sep-alternative, which would have kept `fact ) @` on one line).
* One-line fits follow the engine at 110/73 with the ribbon measured from
  the nest; the closing quote/parens attached after a formula count against
  its last line's fit (probe:q_l5 — 73-content + `"` broke).

## Restriction blocks (R3)

```
restriction Name:
  "formula"                (nest 2; quotes attach directly around the doc)
  // safety formula        (col 2 — iff the formula classifies safety)
<blank>
  /*                       (comment at nest 2)
  expanded formula:
  "formula"
  */
```

* The `axiom` keyword echoes as `restriction` with the identical wrapper
  (probe:q_ax1, target:Cronto_EA).
* The expanded-formula comment content is byte-identical to the statement in
  every observation: predicate expansion happens UPSTREAM of both renderings
  (probe:q_pred1 — an MSR restriction through a predicate shows the
  expansion in BOTH spots; sapic corpus files agree). Rendered as the same
  formula twice.
* Safety classification (pinned by probes q_s1/q_s2 + q_w1 + 84-block corpus
  parity): a formula is safety iff its negation-normal form contains NO
  existential quantifier, msg-sort and temporal alike. Every ⇒-antecedent
  flips polarity; `¬∃` in a conclusion (∀ in NNF) keeps safety, `¬∃` in an
  antecedent (∃ in NNF) defeats it; a non-temporal `∃ y.` conclusion defeats
  it; top-level `¬(∃ …)` restrictions are safety (target:Yubikey).
* Sort-order of the comment lines: statement, safety line, blank, comment —
  the safety line sits BETWEEN the formula and the blank (probe:q_w1 r_eq).

## Lemma blocks (R3)

```
lemma Name [attr1, attr2]:
  all-traces|exists-trace "formula"    (sep at nest 2 — one line iff it fits)
/*                                     (comment at col 0)
guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:      (| all satisfying traces:)
"<guarded block>"                      (opaque input, verbatim lines)
*/
by sorry                               (| the embedded proof, verbatim)
```

* Header: `lemma Name:`; with attributes `lemma Name [` + fill + `]:` — a
  SPACE before `[` (unlike rule attributes), items comma+space separated,
  fill-wrapped aligned after the `[` with `]:` attached to the last item;
  the first item stays beside the `[` even past the width (target:5G_AKA
  weakagreement_… at 120 display columns).
* Attributes render in SOURCE order, duplicates kept — NO canonicalization
  (probe:q_la1 `[hide_lemma=la1, use_induction, hide_lemma=la3, reuse]`).
  Spellings: `sources`, `reuse`, `use_induction`, `hide_lemma=<name>`,
  `heuristic=<value>` with the goal-ranking value verbatim (`S`, `{mytac}` —
  probes q_la2/q_la3; corpus `{sqn}` etc.).
* The trace-quantifier keyword is ALWAYS on the statement line, never the
  header line, even for `"⊤"` (probe:q_w1 l_top).
* Guarded comment at col 0, directly after the statement (no blank line):
  header `guarded formula characterizing all counter-examples:` for
  all-traces, `… all satisfying traces:` for exists-trace (probe:q_w1). The
  quoted content is OPAQUE pre-computed input (the ported guarded transform's
  rendering — note its style differs from the statement printer: n-ary ∧
  rows, lone `∧`/`⇒` lines, parenthesized ∃-body atoms; never produced by
  this crate).
* Failed conversion variant (probe:q_r1): header
  `conversion to guarded formula failed:` followed by the transform's error
  text with every line indented +2 (the raw error spelling, observed
  unindented in the fatal-restriction case, gains exactly 2 columns here).
* Tail: `by sorry` at col 0 when the source had no proof; a source-embedded
  proof re-renders after the `*/` instead (target:Yubikey `induction … qed`)
  — that proof text is PORTED-renderer output, carried verbatim as input.
* Restrictions outside the guarded fragment are a FATAL load error
  (probe:q_p1 raw), so the restriction renderer never sees them; lemmas
  outside it load fine and take the failed-conversion comment (probe:q_r1).
